title: lastrea cristata subsp. uliginosa
intro: Lastrea cristata subsp. uliginosa, also known as the marsh fern or marsh maidenhair fern, is a subspecies of fern native to Europe, western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, its medicinal properties attributed to the presence of various compounds, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Lastrea cristata subsp. uliginosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. It has been traditionally prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, although modern herbal preparations may include extracts or capsules, and should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
